topic: Preference-Based Learning in Audio Applications: A Systematic Analysis
topic_definition: Source-faithful rewrite based only on the original paper's stated eligibility protocol. A paper is eligible if it pertains to preference learning, is applied within the audio domain, and is not a survey or review article. A paper is treated as related to preference learning if it involves the ranking or A/B comparison of two or more audio clips. Numeric ratings are included only when converted to rankings. Preferences may be derived from human judgments or generated synthetically. Works that may not have explicit comparisons are also included when they contain an RL training loop for an audio model. Studies that use preferences solely for evaluation, with no learning component, are excluded. Multimodal works including audio are classified as within the audio domain.
summary: Source-faithful rewrite grounded only in the original paper's stated inclusion protocol and exclusion note.

summary_topics.description: Preference-Based Learning in Audio Applications: A Systematic Analysis

required.criterion: Paper pertains to preference learning, defined as ranking or A/B comparison of two or more audio clips; numeric ratings count only when converted to rankings; preferences may be human or synthetic; papers without explicit comparisons are still eligible when they contain an RL training loop for an audio model.
Paper is applied within the audio domain. Multimodal works including audio are treated as within the audio domain.
Paper is not a survey or review article.

exclusion_criteria.criterion: Studies that use preferences solely for evaluation, with no learning component, are excluded.
Survey or review articles are excluded.
